title: Mark Cuban says he has the solution to growing income inequality, and it's to reward every employee—from CEO to janitor—with company stock | Fortune
excerpt: “I would like to see it so that every single CEO/founder/entrepreneur does what I did, which was to give equity to every single employee," Cuban said.
content_html:
Following SpaceX’s blockbuster $1.77 trillion initial public offering, Juan Hernandez, a former welder for the aerospace and satellite company, saw his wealth balloon overnight as a result of his stake in the firm. Hernandez, who joined SpaceXin 2015 earning $28 per hour, now holds an estimated $880,000 in shares following the company’s IPO, the Wall Street Journal reported. According to billionaire investor Mark Cuban, this model of employees owning shares of the companies they work for should be the norm, not the exception.

Company stock options don’t just offer the possibility of a hefty payday for a business’ perhaps unsung workers, Cuban said; it’s a way to address the pervasive problem of growing income inequality. In a recent episode of the “What It Takes” podcast by Unmoderated News, Cuban outlined his own philosophy around how to close wealth disparities.

“I would like to see it so that every single CEO, founder, entrepreneur does what I did, which was to give equity to every single employee,” he said. “The way you’re going to reduce income inequality for anybody who works with somebody is making sure they get shares of stock and then they benefit.”

Cuban gave 330 employees at his media company Broadcast.com stock ahead of Yahoo acquiring it for $5.7 billion 1999, making 300 of them millionaires, he said. He similarly gave equity and cash bonuses to employees of his first IT consulting company MicroSolutions

This isn’t the first time Cuban has made remarks regarding a more equitable distribution of wealth. Cuban has previously advocated for a $20 federal minimum wage, recalling instances in which a company he invested in, but didn’t run or name, had employees in need of government assistance.

“I made sure they all got raises,” Cuban wrote in an X post. “It was embarrassing to me that we didn’t pay enough. I’ve made, or helped make, at least a thousand millionaires. And I’ll keep working to increase that number.”

The K-shaped economy of the rich getting rich and the poor poorer is reflected in the growing wage gap among U.S. employees: In 2024, S&P 500 CEOS made 285 times more than the median pay of their workers, up from 268 times in 2023, according to a report by the AFL-CIO. The chief executives saw an average compensation of $18.9 million with an average increase of $1.4 million, a 7% year-over-year increase.

Other tech leaders like the world’s richest man Elon Musk, who saw his wealth increase by $215 billion in 2025, have similarly advocated for employee ownership, arguing the strategy aligns company incentives. A little more than a month later, SpaceX’s IPO has already minted at least 4,400 millionaires.

“I’ve always had the philosophy that everyone at the company should receive stock in the company, so that they can participate in the upside of the company,” Musk told Texas Gov. Greg Abbott earlier this month.

How would Cuban’s vision for more company stock ownership play out?

Amid a rise of democratic socialist politicians like New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani championing affordability platforms, Cuban—a believer in “compassionate capitalism”—has argued a free-market economy can generate similar solutions to those generated by government-owned social services. One such example is his launching of the  pharmaceutical company Cost Plus Drugs in 2022, which is able to slash the cost of prescription medications by selling mostly generic products without the typical middlemen of Pharmacy Benefit Managers. 

He’s taking the same mechanism to the income side as well, believing he found a viable way to encourage more companies to adopt his employee-ownership philosophy. For example, Cuban explained, governments can incentivize CEOs to give every employee the same percentage of stock warrants, options, or other equities by dangling the carrot of a lower corporate tax rate than the set 21%. In other words, if a CEO receives a stock valued at 10% of their cash compensation, workers should likewise receive 10% in stock of their own earnings.

“So if the CEO gets $100,000 worth of stock because they make $1 million in cash, and the janitor makes $50,000, then they deserve the same percentage in stock, and that will change the game,” Cuban said.

How viable is Cuban’s philosophy?

Research suggests employee-ownership can be an effective means of closing the wealth gap. A 2021 Harvard Business School study citing government data found that if all private firms in the U.S. became 30% employee-owned, household wealth in the country would effectively double. In contrast, the wealth of the richest Americans would decrease as a result of this model, with the top 1% of wealth holders seeing an average 14% decrease in their net wealth.

Allowing employees to hold company equity is also associated with increased productivity and less turnover, and a 2004 Rutgers University study found companies offering employee ownership stakes of at least 5% have a higher likelihood of survival than those without the benefit, which researchers attributed to greater employment security.

“When you align everyone’s incentives with a common goal, everyone will work harder to achieve that goal,” Ethan Rouen, a Harvard Business School professor of business administration, said in an interview for the college about Harvard’s research. “When you have an equity stake, all of a sudden you have a claim on the upside, and so that incentivizes you to work harder to increase that upside. It is something that has the potential to grow the pie and create wealth for everyone involved.”

blocks.html_en: Following SpaceX’s blockbuster $1.77 trillion initial public offering, Juan Hernandez, a former welder for the aerospace and satellite company, saw his wealth balloon overnight as a result of his stake in the firm. Hernandez, who joined SpaceXin 2015 earning $28 per hour, now holds an estimated $880,000 in shares following the company’s IPO, the Wall Street Journal reported. sentences.en: Following SpaceX’s blockbuster $1.77 trillion initial public offering, Juan Hernandez, a former welder for the aerospace and satellite company, saw his wealth balloon overnight as a result of his stake in the firm.
sentences.zh: 在 SpaceX 大规模首次公开募股 1.77 万亿美元之后，这家航空航天和卫星公司的前焊工胡安·埃尔南德斯 (Juan Hernandez) 由于持有该公司的股份，一夜之间财富暴增。

sentences.en: Hernandez, who joined SpaceXin 2015 earning $28 per hour, now holds an estimated $880,000 in shares following the company’s IPO, the Wall Street Journal reported.
sentences.zh: 据《华尔街日报》报道，埃尔南德斯于 2015 年加入 SpaceX，每小时赚取 28 美元，在该公司 IPO 后，他目前持有估计价值 88 万美元的股票。

sentences.en: According to billionaire investor Mark Cuban, this model of employees owning shares of the companies they work for should be the norm, not the exception.
sentences.zh: 亿万富翁投资者马克·库班表示，这种员工持有其工作公司股份的模式应该是常态，而不是例外。

blocks.html_en: Company stock options don’t just offer the possibility of a hefty payday for a business’ perhaps unsung workers, Cuban said; it’s a way to address the pervasive problem of growing income inequality. In a recent episode of the “What It Takes” podcast by Unmoderated News, Cuban outlined his own philosophy around how to close wealth disparities.

sentences.en: Company stock options don’t just offer the possibility of a hefty payday for a business’ perhaps unsung workers, Cuban said; it’s a way to address the pervasive problem of growing income inequality.
sentences.zh: 库班表示，公司股票期权不仅为企业中可能无名的员工提供了获得高额发薪日的可能性，而且还为他们提供了机会。这是解决日益严重的收入不平等这一普遍问题的一种方法。

sentences.en: In a recent episode of the “What It Takes” podcast by Unmoderated News, Cuban outlined his own philosophy around how to close wealth disparities.
sentences.zh: 在 Unmoderated News 的“What It Takes”播客最近一集中，库班概述了他自己关于如何缩小贫富差距的哲学。

blocks.html_en: “I would like to see it so that every single CEO, founder, entrepreneur does what I did, which was to give equity to every single employee,” he said. “The way you’re going to reduce income inequality for anybody who works with somebody is making sure they get shares of stock and then they benefit.”

sentences.en: “I would like to see it so that every single CEO, founder, entrepreneur does what I did, which was to give equity to every single employee,” he said.
sentences.zh: “我希望看到每一位首席执行官、创始人、企业家都能像我一样，为每一位员工提供股权，”他说。

sentences.en: “The way you’re going to reduce income inequality for anybody who works with somebody is making sure they get shares of stock and then they benefit.”
sentences.zh: “减少与某人合作的任何人的收入不平等的方法是确保他们获得股票，然后他们受益。”

blocks.html_en: Cuban gave 330 employees at his media company Broadcast.com stock ahead of Yahoo acquiring it for $5.7 billion 1999, making 300 of them millionaires, he said. He similarly gave equity and cash bonuses to employees of his first IT consulting company MicroSolutions

sentences.en: Cuban gave 330 employees at his media company Broadcast.com stock ahead of Yahoo acquiring it for $5.7 billion 1999, making 300 of them millionaires, he said.
sentences.zh: 古巴在 1999 年雅虎以 57 亿美元收购其媒体公司 Broadcast.com 之前，向其 330 名员工赠送了股票，使其中 300 人成为百万富翁，他说。

sentences.en: He similarly gave equity and cash bonuses to employees of his first IT consulting company MicroSolutions
sentences.zh: 他同样向他的第一家 IT 咨询公司 MicroSolutions 的员工提供了股权和现金奖金

blocks.html_en: This isn’t the first time Cuban has made remarks regarding a more equitable distribution of wealth. Cuban has previously advocated for a $20 federal minimum wage, recalling instances in which a company he invested in, but didn’t run or name, had employees in need of government assistance.

sentences.en: This isn’t the first time Cuban has made remarks regarding a more equitable distribution of wealth.
sentences.zh: 这并不是库班第一次就更公平的财富分配发表言论。

sentences.en: Cuban has previously advocated for a $20 federal minimum wage, recalling instances in which a company he invested in, but didn’t run or name, had employees in need of government assistance.
sentences.zh: 库班此前曾主张联邦最低工资为 20 美元，他回忆起自己投资的一家公司（但没有经营或命名）的例子，该公司的员工需要政府援助。

blocks.html_en: “I made sure they all got raises,” Cuban wrote in an X post. “It was embarrassing to me that we didn’t pay enough. I’ve made, or helped make, at least a thousand millionaires. And I’ll keep working to increase that number.”

sentences.en: “I made sure they all got raises,” Cuban wrote in an X post.
sentences.zh: “我确保他们都得到加薪，”库班在 X 帖子中写道。

sentences.en: “It was embarrassing to me that we didn’t pay enough.
sentences.zh: “我们付的钱不够，这让我很尴尬。

sentences.en: I’ve made, or helped make, at least a thousand millionaires.
sentences.zh: 我已经创造或帮助创造了至少一千名百万富翁。

sentences.en: And I’ll keep working to increase that number.”
sentences.zh: 我将继续努力增加这个数字。”

blocks.html_en: The K-shaped economy of the rich getting rich and the poor poorer is reflected in the growing wage gap among U.S. employees: In 2024, S&P 500 CEOS made 285 times more than the median pay of their workers, up from 268 times in 2023, according to a report by the AFL-CIO. The chief executives saw an average compensation of $18.9 million with an average increase of $1.4 million, a 7% year-over-year increase.

sentences.en: The K-shaped economy of the rich getting rich and the poor poorer is reflected in the growing wage gap among U.S. employees: In 2024, S&P 500 CEOS made 285 times more than the median pay of their workers, up from 268 times in 2023, according to a report by the AFL-CIO.
sentences.zh: 富者愈富、穷者愈穷的K型经济反映在美国雇员之间日益扩大的工资差距上：根据美国劳工联合会-产联的一份报告，到2024年，标准普尔500强企业首席执行官的工资是其工人工资中位数的285倍，而2023年为268倍。

sentences.en: The chief executives saw an average compensation of $18.9 million with an average increase of $1.4 million, a 7% year-over-year increase.
sentences.zh: 首席执行官的平均薪酬为1890万美元，平均增加140万美元，同比增长7%。

blocks.html_en: Other tech leaders like the world’s richest man Elon Musk, who saw his wealth increase by $215 billion in 2025, have similarly advocated for employee ownership, arguing the strategy aligns company incentives. A little more than a month later, SpaceX’s IPO has already minted at least 4,400 millionaires.

sentences.en: Other tech leaders like the world’s richest man Elon Musk, who saw his wealth increase by $215 billion in 2025, have similarly advocated for employee ownership, arguing the strategy aligns company incentives.
sentences.zh: 其他科技领袖，例如世界首富埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk)，他的财富在 2025 年增加了 2150 亿美元，他也同样提倡员工持股，认为该战略与公司激励措施相一致。

sentences.en: A little more than a month later, SpaceX’s IPO has already minted at least 4,400 millionaires.
sentences.zh: 一个多月后，SpaceX 的 IPO 已经创造了至少 4,400 名百万富翁。

blocks.html_en: “I’ve always had the philosophy that everyone at the company should receive stock in the company, so that they can participate in the upside of the company,” Musk told Texas Gov. Greg Abbott earlier this month.

sentences.en: “I’ve always had the philosophy that everyone at the company should receive stock in the company, so that they can participate in the upside of the company,” Musk told Texas Gov.
sentences.zh: “我一直有一个理念，即公司的每个人都应该获得公司的股票，这样他们就可以分享公司的发展成果，”马斯克告诉德克萨斯州州长。

sentences.en: Greg Abbott earlier this month.
sentences.zh: 本月早些时候，格雷格·阿博特。

blocks.html_en: How would Cuban’s vision for more company stock ownership play out?

sentences.en: How would Cuban’s vision for more company stock ownership play out?
sentences.zh: 库班关于更多公司股权的愿景将如何实现？

blocks.html_en: Amid a rise of democratic socialist politicians like New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani championing affordability platforms, Cuban—a believer in “compassionate capitalism”—has argued a free-market economy can generate similar solutions to those generated by government-owned social services. One such example is his launching of the  pharmaceutical company Cost Plus Drugs in 2022, which is able to slash the cost of prescription medications by selling mostly generic products without the typical middlemen of Pharmacy Benefit Managers.

sentences.en: Amid a rise of democratic socialist politicians like New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani championing affordability platforms, Cuban—a believer in “compassionate capitalism”—has argued a free-market economy can generate similar solutions to those generated by government-owned social services.
sentences.zh: 在纽约市市长佐兰·马姆达尼（Zohran Mamdani）等支持负担得起平台的民主社会主义政客崛起的背景下，“富有同情心的资本主义”的信徒库班认为，自由市场经济可以产生与政府拥有的社会服务类似的解决方案。

sentences.en: One such example is his launching of the pharmaceutical company Cost Plus Drugs in 2022, which is able to slash the cost of prescription medications by selling mostly generic products without the typical middlemen of Pharmacy Benefit Managers.
sentences.zh: 其中一个例子是他在 2022 年创办了 Cost Plus Drugs 制药公司，该公司能够通过销售大部分非专利产品来削减处方药的成本，而无需典型的药品福利经理中间商。

blocks.html_en: He’s taking the same mechanism to the income side as well, believing he found a viable way to encourage more companies to adopt his employee-ownership philosophy. For example, Cuban explained, governments can incentivize CEOs to give every employee the same percentage of stock warrants, options, or other equities by dangling the carrot of a lower corporate tax rate than the set 21%. In other words, if a CEO receives a stock valued at 10% of their cash compensation, workers should likewise receive 10% in stock of their own earnings.

sentences.en: He’s taking the same mechanism to the income side as well, believing he found a viable way to encourage more companies to adopt his employee-ownership philosophy.
sentences.zh: 他也在收入方面采取了同样的机制，相信他找到了一种可行的方法来鼓励更多公司采用他的员工持股理念。

sentences.en: For example, Cuban explained, governments can incentivize CEOs to give every employee the same percentage of stock warrants, options, or other equities by dangling the carrot of a lower corporate tax rate than the set 21%.
sentences.zh: 例如，库班解释说，政府可以通过低于设定的 21% 的企业税率来激励首席执行官为每位员工提供相同比例的认股权证、期权或其他股权。

sentences.en: In other words, if a CEO receives a stock valued at 10% of their cash compensation, workers should likewise receive 10% in stock of their own earnings.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，如果首席执行官收到的股票价值相当于其现金薪酬的 10%，那么员工也应该收到相当于自己收入 10% 的股票。

blocks.html_en: “So if the CEO gets $100,000 worth of stock because they make $1 million in cash, and the janitor makes $50,000, then they deserve the same percentage in stock, and that will change the game,” Cuban said.

sentences.en: “So if the CEO gets $100,000 worth of stock because they make $1 million in cash, and the janitor makes $50,000, then they deserve the same percentage in stock, and that will change the game,” Cuban said.
sentences.zh: 库班说：“因此，如果首席执行官因为赚了 100 万美元现金而获得了价值 10 万美元的股票，而看门人赚了 5 万美元，那么他们应该获得相同比例的股票，这将改变游戏规则。”

sentences.en: How viable is Cuban’s philosophy?
sentences.zh: 库班的哲学有多可行？

blocks.html_en: Research suggests employee-ownership can be an effective means of closing the wealth gap. A 2021 Harvard Business School study citing government data found that if all private firms in the U.S. became 30% employee-owned, household wealth in the country would effectively double. In contrast, the wealth of the richest Americans would decrease as a result of this model, with the top 1% of wealth holders seeing an average 14% decrease in their net wealth.

sentences.en: Research suggests employee-ownership can be an effective means of closing the wealth gap.
sentences.zh: 研究表明，员工持股可以成为缩小贫富差距的有效手段。

sentences.en: A 2021 Harvard Business School study citing government data found that if all private firms in the U.S. became 30% employee-owned, household wealth in the country would effectively double.
sentences.zh: 哈佛商学院 2021 年的一项研究援引政府数据发现，如果美国所有私营企业 30% 的股份由员工持股，该国的家庭财富实际上将翻倍。

sentences.en: In contrast, the wealth of the richest Americans would decrease as a result of this model, with the top 1% of wealth holders seeing an average 14% decrease in their net wealth.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，美国最富有的人的财富会因为这种模式而减少，最富有的 1% 的财富持有者的净财富平均减少 14%。

blocks.html_en: Allowing employees to hold company equity is also associated with increased productivity and less turnover, and a 2004 Rutgers University study found companies offering employee ownership stakes of at least 5% have a higher likelihood of survival than those without the benefit, which researchers attributed to greater employment security.

sentences.en: Allowing employees to hold company equity is also associated with increased productivity and less turnover, and a 2004 Rutgers University study found companies offering employee ownership stakes of at least 5% have a higher likelihood of survival than those without the benefit, which researchers attributed to greater employment security.
sentences.zh: 允许员工持有公司股权还与提高生产率和减少人员流动有关，罗格斯大学 2004 年的一项研究发现，为员工提供至少 5% 股权的公司比没有这种福利的公司生存的可能性更高，研究人员将此归因于更大的就业保障。

blocks.html_en: “When you align everyone’s incentives with a common goal, everyone will work harder to achieve that goal,” Ethan Rouen, a Harvard Business School professor of business administration, said in an interview for the college about Harvard’s research. “When you have an equity stake, all of a sudden you have a claim on the upside, and so that incentivizes you to work harder to increase that upside. It is something that has the potential to grow the pie and create wealth for everyone involved.”

sentences.en: “When you align everyone’s incentives with a common goal, everyone will work harder to achieve that goal,” Ethan Rouen, a Harvard Business School professor of business administration, said in an interview for the college about Harvard’s research.
sentences.zh: 哈佛商学院工商管理学教授伊森·鲁恩（Ethan Rouen）在接受该学院采访时谈到哈佛的研究时表示：“当你将每个人的激励措施与一个共同目标结合起来时，每个人都会更加努力地工作以实现该目标。”

sentences.en: “When you have an equity stake, all of a sudden you have a claim on the upside, and so that incentivizes you to work harder to increase that upside.
sentences.zh: “当你拥有股权时，突然间你就拥有了上涨空间，这会激励你更加努力地工作以增加这种上涨空间。

sentences.en: It is something that has the potential to grow the pie and create wealth for everyone involved.”
sentences.zh: 它有潜力做大蛋糕，为每个参与者创造财富。”

content_html_zh:
在 SpaceX 大规模首次公开募股 1.77 万亿美元之后，这家航空航天和卫星公司的前焊工胡安·埃尔南德斯 (Juan Hernandez) 由于持有该公司的股份，一夜之间财富暴增。 据《华尔街日报》报道，埃尔南德斯于 2015 年加入 SpaceX，每小时赚取 28 美元，在该公司 IPO 后，他目前持有估计价值 88 万美元的股票。 亿万富翁投资者马克·库班表示，这种员工持有其工作公司股份的模式应该是常态，而不是例外。

库班表示，公司股票期权不仅为企业中可能无名的员工提供了获得高额发薪日的可能性，而且还为他们提供了机会。这是解决日益严重的收入不平等这一普遍问题的一种方法。 在 Unmoderated News 的“What It Takes”播客最近一集中，库班概述了他自己关于如何缩小贫富差距的哲学。

“我希望看到每一位首席执行官、创始人、企业家都能像我一样，为每一位员工提供股权，”他说。 “减少与某人合作的任何人的收入不平等的方法是确保他们获得股票，然后他们受益。”

古巴在 1999 年雅虎以 57 亿美元收购其媒体公司 Broadcast.com 之前，向其 330 名员工赠送了股票，使其中 300 人成为百万富翁，他说。 他同样向他的第一家 IT 咨询公司 MicroSolutions 的员工提供了股权和现金奖金

这并不是库班第一次就更公平的财富分配发表言论。 库班此前曾主张联邦最低工资为 20 美元，他回忆起自己投资的一家公司（但没有经营或命名）的例子，该公司的员工需要政府援助。

“我确保他们都得到加薪，”库班在 X 帖子中写道。 “我们付的钱不够，这让我很尴尬。 我已经创造或帮助创造了至少一千名百万富翁。 我将继续努力增加这个数字。”

富者愈富、穷者愈穷的K型经济反映在美国雇员之间日益扩大的工资差距上：根据美国劳工联合会-产联的一份报告，到2024年，标准普尔500强企业首席执行官的工资是其工人工资中位数的285倍，而2023年为268倍。 首席执行官的平均薪酬为1890万美元，平均增加140万美元，同比增长7%。

其他科技领袖，例如世界首富埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk)，他的财富在 2025 年增加了 2150 亿美元，他也同样提倡员工持股，认为该战略与公司激励措施相一致。 一个多月后，SpaceX 的 IPO 已经创造了至少 4,400 名百万富翁。

“我一直有一个理念，即公司的每个人都应该获得公司的股票，这样他们就可以分享公司的发展成果，”马斯克告诉德克萨斯州州长。 本月早些时候，格雷格·阿博特。

库班关于更多公司股权的愿景将如何实现？

在纽约市市长佐兰·马姆达尼（Zohran Mamdani）等支持负担得起平台的民主社会主义政客崛起的背景下，“富有同情心的资本主义”的信徒库班认为，自由市场经济可以产生与政府拥有的社会服务类似的解决方案。 其中一个例子是他在 2022 年创办了 Cost Plus Drugs 制药公司，该公司能够通过销售大部分非专利产品来削减处方药的成本，而无需典型的药品福利经理中间商。

他也在收入方面采取了同样的机制，相信他找到了一种可行的方法来鼓励更多公司采用他的员工持股理念。 例如，库班解释说，政府可以通过低于设定的 21% 的企业税率来激励首席执行官为每位员工提供相同比例的认股权证、期权或其他股权。 换句话说，如果首席执行官收到的股票价值相当于其现金薪酬的 10%，那么员工也应该收到相当于自己收入 10% 的股票。

库班说：“因此，如果首席执行官因为赚了 100 万美元现金而获得了价值 10 万美元的股票，而看门人赚了 5 万美元，那么他们应该获得相同比例的股票，这将改变游戏规则。”

库班的哲学有多可行？

研究表明，员工持股可以成为缩小贫富差距的有效手段。 哈佛商学院 2021 年的一项研究援引政府数据发现，如果美国所有私营企业 30% 的股份由员工持股，该国的家庭财富实际上将翻倍。 相比之下，美国最富有的人的财富会因为这种模式而减少，最富有的 1% 的财富持有者的净财富平均减少 14%。

允许员工持有公司股权还与提高生产率和减少人员流动有关，罗格斯大学 2004 年的一项研究发现，为员工提供至少 5% 股权的公司比没有这种福利的公司生存的可能性更高，研究人员将此归因于更大的就业保障。

哈佛商学院工商管理学教授伊森·鲁恩（Ethan Rouen）在接受该学院采访时谈到哈佛的研究时表示：“当你将每个人的激励措施与一个共同目标结合起来时，每个人都会更加努力地工作以实现该目标。” “当你拥有股权时，突然间你就拥有了上涨空间，这会激励你更加努力地工作以增加这种上涨空间。 它有潜力做大蛋糕，为每个参与者创造财富。”
